commands.review_summary:
The PR routes supervisor child spawns through the shared Windows spawn resolver, adding cwd and child-env PATH/PATHEXT-aware .cmd/.bat handling plus focused tests and a plugin SDK API baseline refresh.

PR surface: Source +70, Tests +130, Generated 0. Total +200 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main still passes bare `claude` through the supervisor child adapter, Node documents that `.cmd` files need shell mediation on Windows, and recent Windows comments report the same ENOENT failure.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are maintainer policy acceptance for no-PATH fail-closed behavior plus a dirty rebase/baseline refresh, not a safe autonomous repair lane.

target.title: fix: resolve Windows .cmd shims to underlying .exe before spawn
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes Windows supervisor child spawns for npm-style `.cmd`/`.bat` shims such as `claude.cmd`.
- Routes supervisor child argv through the shared Windows spawn resolver in `src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.ts` instead of adding a process-local resolver or a hardcoded Claude allowlist.
- Resolves relative shim paths from the child `cwd`, honors the provided child env `PATH`/`PATHEXT`, and fails closed when Windows gets a bare command with an explicit empty or missing `PATH`.
- Preserves existing supervisor env conversion, Linux OOM wrapping, stdin handling, Windows output decoding, and process-tree kill behavior.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #68788
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

`claude-cli` reaches the process supervisor as a bare command that resolves to a Windows npm shim. The old child adapter had its own narrow Windows command handling, so it could either miss the shim entirely or spawn a `.cmd`/`.bat` target directly. That duplicated logic also did not cover relative shim paths from the child `cwd`.

The fix moves the supervisor child adapter onto the same shared Windows spawn policy used by other process paths. The shared resolver now accepts `cwd`, uses the child env's own `PATH`/`PATHEXT`, resolves npm-style `.cmd`/`.bat` shims to `process.execPath <entrypoint>`, and rejects unresolved bare Windows commands when the explicit child env has no usable `PATH`.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Windows supervisor child spawns can launch PATH-resolved npm-style CLI shims without direct `.cmd`/`.bat` spawning.
- Relative shim paths resolve from the configured child `cwd`.
- Explicit Windows child envs without `PATH` now reject unresolved bare commands instead of letting process creation search an unintended parent PATH.
- Non-Windows behavior is unchanged.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, narrowly.
- Data access scope changed? No.

The command execution change is bounded to Windows spawn preparation. It reuses the existing shared npm-shim parser, does not add a provider-specific allowlist, and fail-closes explicit no-`PATH` child envs instead of widening lookup to the parent process environment.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
- Target tests:
  - `src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts`
  - `src/process/supervisor/adapters/child.test.ts`
  - `src/process/windows-command.test.ts`
- Scenarios locked in:
  - Shared Windows resolver resolves relative `.cmd` shims from caller `cwd`.
  - Shared Windows resolver does not inspect bare missing `.cmd` files from process `cwd`.
  - Shared Windows resolver does not inherit parent `PATH` when the caller supplied an explicit env without `PATH`.
  - Supervisor child adapter resolves a Windows `claude.cmd` shim through env `PATH` to `process.execPath <entrypoint>.js`.
  - Supervisor child adapter rejects bare Windows commands when child env `PATH` is empty.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Windows supervisor child spawns now route PATH-resolved `.cmd`/`.bat` shims through the shared Windows spawn resolver instead of direct `.cmd`/`.bat` spawn. This fixes `claude-cli` and similar npm-style CLI shims without hardcoding Claude.

Real environment tested: native Windows GitHub runner, GitHub-hosted Windows WSL2 runner, and AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts src/process/supervisor/adapters/child.test.ts src/process/windows-command.test.ts`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.ts src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts src/process/supervisor/adapters/child.ts src/process/supervisor/adapters/child.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base origin/main`
- Native Windows CI: run `26693645026`, job `78674317051`, check `checks-windows-node-test`
- GitHub-hosted Windows WSL2 probe: run `26693667099`, job `78674362346`
- AWS Crabbox changed gate: run `run_03d7d7ff9d2a`, lease `cbx_7ca3ef524df7`, command `pnpm check:changed`

Evidence after fix:

- Focused local Vitest shards passed for the shared resolver, supervisor child adapter, and existing Windows command tests.
- Plugin SDK API baseline check passed.
- Targeted oxlint and whitespace checks passed.
- Autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings after the explicit no-`PATH` Windows env fix.
- Native Windows CI `checks-windows-node-test` passed on head `416bdb79f2a435ea5f1f052fe640dde2b7257b66`.
- WSL2 probe imported `UbuntuProbe`, reported `PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 24.04 LTS"`, ran under Microsoft WSL2 kernel `6.6.114.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2`, and exited successfully.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` completed successfully, exit `0`.

Observed result after fix: supervisor child spawn preparation materializes npm-style Windows shims to `process.execPath <shim entrypoint>` before spawn; relative shim paths resolve from child `cwd`; explicit envs without `PATH` fail closed for bare commands.

What was not tested: live Claude/Discord credentials. Azure native Windows Crabbox did not lease within five minutes, and Blacksmith's Windows runner lacks Hyper-V/VirtualMachinePlatform for WSL2, so native Windows proof came from PR CI and WSL2 proof came from GitHub-hosted `windows-2025`.

## Compatibility / Migration

Backward compatible for configured PATH environments. No config migration.
target.author: haseebdev
